creator: dewayne@heuristicsystems.com.au
text:
(In reply to Samee Shahzada from comment #0)
The problem remains, on FreeBSD 11.1 Stable both i386 and amd64 via portmaster.
/usr/local/bin/ccache /usr/local/libexec/ccache/cc -O2 -pipe -DOPENSSL_NO_SSL2 -DOPENSSL_NO_SSL3 -g0 -ggdb0 -DSTRIP_FBSDID -UDEBUGGING -UDEBUG -march=c3-2 -mtune=c3-2  -I/usr/local/include -fno-strict-aliasing -DOPENSSL_NO_SSL2 -DOPENSSL_NO_SSL3 -g0 -ggdb0 -DSTRIP_FBSDID -UDEBUGGING -UDEBUG -march=c3-2 -mtune=c3-2 -Wall -Wstrict-prototypes -I/var/ports/usr/ports/mail/spamd/work/spamd-4.9.1/spamlogd/../spamd    -MD  -MF.depend.spamlogd.o -MTspamlogd.o -std=gnu99    -Qunused-arguments  -c spamlogd.c -o spamlogd.o
spamlogd.c:169:32: error: use of undeclared identifier 'BIOCLOCK'
        if (ioctl(pcap_fileno(hpcap), BIOCLOCK) < 0) {
                                      ^
1 error generated.
*** Error code 1

Stop.
make[2]: stopped in /var/ports/usr/ports/mail/spamd/work/spamd-4.9.1/spamlogd
*** Error code 1

# find /usr/include/ |xargs grep BIOCLOCK
/usr/include/net/bpf.h:#define  BIOCLOCK        _IO('B', 122)
